questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of neutral oxides among the following oxides is $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_3, \mathrm{NO}_2, \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5, \mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{CO}_2, \mathrm{SO}_2$ and $\mathrm{CrO}_3$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}$, NO and CO are neutral oxides remaining are acidic oxides.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In how many of the following salts, IUPAC name ends with 'ate'.
\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{KClO}_2 & \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_2\right)_2, & \mathrm{CaCO}_3 \\\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3, & \mathrm{CaC}_2 \mathrm{O}_4, & KSCN \\\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{HPO}_3, & \mathrm{Ca}_3\left(\mathrm{PO}_4\right)_2, & \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{ZnO}_2\end{array}
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following oxy anions
$\mathrm{PO}_4^{3-}, \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6^{4-}, \mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}, \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}, \mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}, \mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_5^{2-}, \mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$
and find the value of $R+Q-P$
Where
$\mathrm{P}=$ Number of oxy anions having three equivalent
$\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{O}$ bond per central atom
Q = Number of oxy anions having two equivalent
X - O bonds per central atom
$R=$ Number of oxy anions having four equivalent
X - O bonds per central atom
Consider the following complex: $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5 \mathrm{CO}_3\right] \mathrm{ClO}_4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of compounds among the following in which the sulphur has +6 oxidation state is $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_5, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_4, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_6, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_8, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_3$
questions.solution: In the following compound sulphur has +6 oxidation state $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_5, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_8$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
How many of the following liberate coloured vapours/gas with concentrated $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ ?
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{KCl}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{KNO}_2(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{KI}(\mathrm{s}), \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{KCl}(\mathrm{s}) \\ & \mathrm{KBr}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{MnO}_2(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{KNO}_3(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{KCl}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{MnO}_2(\mathrm{~s}), \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_3\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
How many of the following produce $\mathrm{N}_2$ on being heated?
(a) $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
(b) $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_2$
(c) $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_3$
(d) $\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{N}_3\right)_2$
(e) $\mathrm{NaN}_3$
(f) $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{ClO}_4$
(g) $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$
(h) $\mathrm{Pb}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: How many equivalent $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds are there in $\mathrm{Cl}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ ?
questions.solution:
The structure of $\mathrm{Cl}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ is
The number of equivalent $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds $=6$.
